channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Bengal Election Officer Transferred After Dispute Involving Officer Nicknamed 'Singham'
item.description: The Election Commission of India has decided to transfer the polling officer in West Bengal following a recent controversy involving a government official who has been referred to in media reports as ...

item.title: PSG defeats Bayern Munich 5-4 in high-scoring Champions League semifinal first leg
item.description: In a thrilling and chaotic first leg of the UEFA Champions League semifinal at Parc des Princes, Paris Saint-Germain defeated Bayern Munich 5-4 on Tuesday, April 28, 2026. The match featured nine goal...

item.title: Queensland Government Pushes for New Oil Refinery in Gladstone Amid Ongoing Fuel Crisis
item.description: The Queensland Government is actively pursuing the development of a new oil refinery in the Gladstone region as Australia grapples with a persistent national fuel crisis. Premier David Crisafulli has ...

item.title: India experiences rising night-time temperatures amid ongoing heatwave
item.description: India is witnessing unusually high night-time temperatures as a severe heatwave continues to grip many regions. Experts warn that this phenomenon, often referred to as 'hot nights', is becoming more f...

item.title: King Charles emphasises U.S.-U.K. relations amid global uncertainties
item.description: On April 28, 2026, King Charles III addressed a joint session of the United States Congress, marking a significant moment in the ongoing diplomatic relations between the United Kingdom and the United ...

item.title: US Influencer Kiara Brokenbrough Dies During Childbirth, Son Survives
item.description: Kiara Brokenbrough, a 32-year-old influencer from the US, tragically passed away during childbirth, leaving her husband Joel and their newborn son Jonah in shock. The baby survived and is currently re...

item.title: Deborah Mailman reflects on Indigenous stories, grief and resilience in Wolfram
item.description: Deborah Mailman sat on Arrernte Country in Central Australia while filming Wolfram, Warwick Thornton's frontier western set in the 1930s. She plays Pansy, an Indigenous mother whose children are stole...

item.title: Trump and top officials gathered at gala without designated survivor, raising succession concerns
item.description: During the White House Correspondents' Association dinner, US President Donald Trump and at least 13 high-ranking officials from the presidential line of succession were present, including the vice pr...

item.title: Ogu-Bolo Council Chairman Presents N20.51 Billion 2026 Budget Proposal
item.description: The Ogu-Bolo Local Government Chairman has presented the area council’s 2026 budget proposal worth N20.51 billion, outlining the administration’s spending priorities for the coming fiscal year. The pr...

item.title: Top Internet Providers for Remote Work in Nigeria and Their Plans
item.description: For people wey dey work from home for Nigeria, finding stable internet fit sometimes be wahala. Dis article break down the main internet service providers (ISPs) wey dey reliable for remote work. Spec...

item.title: Brothers deny assaulting police officers during Manchester Airport incident
item.description: At Liverpool Crown Court, Mohammed Fahir Amaaz, 21, and his brother Muhammad Amaad, 26, stand accused of assaulting police officers at Manchester Airport in July 2024. The incident reportedly occurred...

item.title: Jomo Kenyatta Lookalike Plans to Meet Uhuru Kenyatta, Encourages Youth Protesters
item.description: Michael Ngige, a 62-year-old Kenyan who bears a striking resemblance to Mzee Jomo Kenyatta, recently returned from the United States and has captured attention across the country. Despite having no fa...

item.title: Lagos APC Governorship Aspirant Ajose Challenges Hamzat’s Push for Consensus Ahead of Primaries
item.description: For Lagos State, politics don dey heat as Samuel Ajose, one APC governorship aspirant, don call out Deputy Governor Obafemi Hamzat. Ajose accuse Hamzat say him dey try force consensus candidacy before...

item.title: BJP achieves comprehensive victory in Gujarat local body elections winning all 15 municipal corporations
item.description: In a significant show of strength, the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) has once again demonstrated its strong hold over Gujarat by securing a sweeping victory in the recent municipal and local body polls...

item.title: Testing and Commissioning Delays Gordie Howe Bridge Opening
item.description: The Gordie Howe International Bridge, intended to connect Windsor, Ontario, with Detroit, Michigan, has yet to open due to extensive testing and commissioning requirements. Phil Saoud, vice-president ...

item.title: Africa’s Digital Growth Highlights Challenges in Internet Access and Security
item.description: Across Africa, digital connectivity is rapidly reshaping the economy and daily life. Countries such as South Africa, Kenya, Nigeria, and Ghana have become hubs for digital innovation, with mobile bank...

item.title: MIT develops self-organizing laser beam for faster 3D brain imaging
item.description: Researchers at MIT have discovered that under specific conditions, normally chaotic laser light can spontaneously organize into a narrow, highly focused 'pencil beam.' This breakthrough allows for rap...

item.title: James Comey indicted over Instagram post allegedly threatening President Trump
item.description: Former FBI Director James Comey has been indicted by Donald Trump's Justice Department over an Instagram post allegedly implying a threat to the President's life. The post, published last May, feature...

item.title: Decline in small-scale property investors and its potential effects on New Zealand's housing market
item.description: In New Zealand, 'mum and dad' investors have long played a significant role in the residential property market, often accounting for a large share of sales and contributing to rising house prices thro...

item.title: Sunday Times editor on special leave pending SIU investigation into lottery fund mismanagement
item.description: Makhudu Sefara, editor of the Sunday Times, has been placed on special leave after being linked to an investigation by the Special Investigating Unit (SIU) concerning misappropriated lottery funds. Se...
